---
title: "Solutions — Module 1: R Foundations for Biological Data"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 2
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
library(tidyverse)
set.seed(42)
```

# Solution 1.1 — Vectors and factors

```{r sol-1-1}
expr_class <- c("high", "low", "medium", "high", "high",
                "low", "medium", "low", "high", "medium")

expr_factor <- factor(expr_class, levels = c("low", "medium", "high"))
print(expr_factor)

table(expr_factor)
```

The levels are printed in the specified order: low, medium, high. `table()` respects factor level ordering.

# Solution 1.2 — Tibbles and data types

```{r sol-1-2}
patients <- tibble(
  patientID = c("pt001", "pt002", "pt003", "pt004", "pt005"),
  age       = c(52L, 67L, 45L, 58L, 71L),
  treatment = c("A", "B", "A", "B", "A"),
  response  = c(TRUE, FALSE, TRUE, TRUE, FALSE)
)

sapply(patients, class)

patients <- patients %>% mutate(treatment = factor(treatment))

patients %>% filter(age > 55, treatment == "A")
```

# Solution 1.3 — File reading

```{r sol-1-3}
library(here)
clinical <- read_csv(here("data", "clinical.csv"), show_col_types = FALSE)

colnames(clinical)
nrow(clinical)

clinical %>% select(where(is.numeric)) %>% slice_head(n = 5)
```

# Solution 1.4 — Functions

```{r sol-1-4}
summarise_vector <- function(x) {
  list(
    n         = length(x),
    mean      = mean(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd        = sd(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    min       = min(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    max       = max(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_missing = sum(is.na(x))
  )
}

test_vec <- rnorm(20)
test_vec[c(3, 11, 17)] <- NA

summarise_vector(test_vec)
```

# Solution 1.5 — String work

```{r sol-1-5}
probes <- c("200008_s_at", "200052_s_at", "1405_i_at", "200600_at", "200604_s_at")

sum(str_detect(probes, "_s_at"))

str_extract(probes, "^[0-9]+")

sort(probes)
```

`str_extract("^[0-9]+")` captures the run of digits before the first underscore. `1405_i_at` sorts first alphabetically because `1` < `2`.
